message:
fix(bytecode): Add bool constant and PyObject extraction support

Two fixes for eval() on boolean expressions like ~False:

1. Bytecode compiler (src/codegen/bytecode.zig):
   - Add .bool variant to Constant union
   - Handle .bool in constant compilation and serialization
   - Type tag 3 matches runtime's bytecode format

2. toPyValue PyObject handling (packages/runtime/src/Objects/object.zig):
   - Detect *PyObject pointers from BytecodeVM.execute()
   - Extract actual values using PyLong_Check, PyFloat_Check, etc.
   - Convert to proper PyValue (.int, .float, .bool, .string)
   - Enables assertEqual(vm_result, primitive) comparisons

This fixes eval("~False") == -1 which was failing because:
- Bytecode compiler rejected bool constants with UnsupportedConstant
- VM result (*PyObject) wasn't being unwrapped for comparison
